NKJV.1: A Song. A Psalm of the sons of Korah. Great is the LORD, and greatly to be praised In the city of our God, In His holy mountain.
NKJV.2: Beautiful in elevation, The joy of the whole earth, Is Mount Zion on the sides of the north, The city of the great King.
NKJV.3: God is in her palaces; He is known as her refuge.
NKJV.4: For behold, the kings assembled, They passed by together.
NKJV.5: They saw it, and so they marveled; They were troubled, they hastened away.
NKJV.6: Fear took hold of them there, And pain, as of a woman in birth pangs,
NKJV.7: As when You break the ships of Tarshish With an east wind.
NKJV.8: As we have heard, So we have seen In the city of the LORD of hosts, In the city of our God: God will establish it forever. Selah
NKJV.9: We have thought, O God, on Your lovingkindness, In the midst of Your temple.
NKJV.10: According to Your name, O God, So is Your praise to the ends of the earth; Your right hand is full of righteousness.
NKJV.11: Let Mount Zion rejoice, Let the daughters of Judah be glad, Because of Your judgments.
NKJV.12: Walk about Zion, And go all around her. Count her towers;
NKJV.13: Mark well her bulwarks; Consider her palaces; That you may tell it to the generation following.
NKJV.14: For this is God, Our God forever and ever; He will be our guide Even to death.

ESV.1: Great is the LORD and greatly to be praised in the city of our God! His holy mountain,
ESV.2: beautiful in elevation, is the joy of all the earth, Mount Zion, in the far north, the city of the great King.
ESV.3: Within her citadels God has made himself known as a fortress.
ESV.4: For behold, the kings assembled; they came on together.
ESV.5: As soon as they saw it, they were astounded; they were in panic; they took to flight.
ESV.6: Trembling took hold of them there, anguish as of a woman in labor.
ESV.7: By the east wind you shattered the ships of Tarshish.
ESV.8: As we have heard, so have we seen in the city of the LORD of hosts, in the city of our God, which God will establish forever. Selah
ESV.9: We have thought on your steadfast love, O God, in the midst of your temple.
ESV.10: As your name, O God, so your praise reaches to the ends of the earth. Your right hand is filled with righteousness.
ESV.11: Let Mount Zion be glad! Let the daughters of Judah rejoice because of your judgments!
ESV.12: Walk about Zion, go around her, number her towers,
ESV.13: consider well her ramparts, go through her citadels, that you may tell the next generation
ESV.14: that this is God, our God forever and ever. He will guide us forever.

NASB.1: A Song; a Psalm of the sons of Korah. Great is the LORD, and greatly to be praised, In the city of our God, His holy mountain.
NASB.2: Beautiful in elevation, the joy of the whole earth, Is Mount Zion [in] the far north, The city of the great King.
NASB.3: God, in her palaces, Has made Himself known as a stronghold.
NASB.4: For, lo, the kings assembled themselves, They passed by together.
NASB.5: They saw [it], then they were amazed; They were terrified, they fled in alarm.
NASB.6: Panic seized them there, Anguish, as of a woman in childbirth.
NASB.7: With the east wind You break the ships of Tarshish.
NASB.8: As we have heard, so have we seen In the city of the LORD of hosts, in the city of our God; God will establish her forever. Selah.
NASB.9: We have thought on Your lovingkindness, O God, In the midst of Your temple.
NASB.10: As is Your name, O God, So is Your praise to the ends of the earth; Your right hand is full of righteousness.
NASB.11: Let Mount Zion be glad, Let the daughters of Judah rejoice Because of Your judgments.
NASB.12: Walk about Zion and go around her; Count her towers;
NASB.13: Consider her ramparts; Go through her palaces, That you may tell [it] to the next generation.
NASB.14: For such is God, Our God forever and ever; He will guide us until death.

NLT.1:
How great is the LORD,
how deserving of praise,
in the city of our God,
which sits on his holy mountain!
NLT.2:
It is high and magnificent;
the whole earth rejoices to see it!
Mount Zion, the holy mountain,
is the city of the great King!
NLT.3:
God himself is in Jerusalem’s towers,
revealing himself as its defender.
NLT.4:
The kings of the earth joined forces
and advanced against the city.
NLT.5:
But when they saw it, they were stunned;
they were terrified and ran away.
NLT.6:
They were gripped with terror
and writhed in pain like a woman in labor.
NLT.7:
You destroyed them like the mighty ships of Tarshish
shattered by a powerful east wind.
NLT.8:
We had heard of the city’s glory,
but now we have seen it ourselves —
the city of the LORD of Heaven’s Armies.
It is the city of our God;
he will make it safe forever. Interlude
NLT.9:
O God, we meditate on your unfailing love
as we worship in your Temple.
NLT.10:
As your name deserves, O God,
you will be praised to the ends of the earth.
Your strong right hand is filled with victory.
NLT.11:
Let the people on Mount Zion rejoice.
Let all the towns of Judah be glad
because of your justice.
NLT.12:
Go, inspect the city of Jerusalem.
Walk around and count the many towers.
NLT.13:
Take note of the fortified walls,
and tour all the citadels,
that you may describe them
to future generations.
NLT.14:
For that is what God is like.
He is our God forever and ever,
and he will guide us until we die.

WEB.1: A Song. A Psalm by the sons of Korah. Great is Yahweh, and greatly to be praised, in the city of our God, in his holy mountain.
WEB.2: Beautiful in elevation, the joy of the whole earth, is Mount Zion, on the north sides, the city of the great King.
WEB.3: God has shown himself in her citadels as a refuge.
WEB.4: For, behold, the kings assembled themselves, they passed by together.
WEB.5: They saw it, then they were amazed. They were dismayed. They hurried away.
WEB.6: Trembling took hold of them there, pain, as of a woman in travail.
WEB.7: With the east wind, you break the ships of Tarshish.
WEB.8: As we have heard, so we have seen, in the city of Yahweh of Armies, in the city of our God. God will establish it forever. Selah.
WEB.9: We have thought about your loving kindness, God, in the middle of your temple.
WEB.10: As is your name, God, so is your praise to the ends of the earth. Your right hand is full of righteousness.
WEB.11: Let Mount Zion be glad! Let the daughters of Judah rejoice, Because of your judgments.
WEB.12: Walk about Zion, and go around her. Number its towers.
WEB.13: Mark well her bulwarks. Consider her palaces, that you may tell it to the next generation.
WEB.14: For this God is our God forever and ever. He will be our guide even to death.

CSB17.1:
A song. A psalm of the sons of Korah. [1] The LORD is great and highly praised
in the city of our God. ⓖ
His holy mountain,
CSB17.2:
rising splendidly,
is the joy of the whole earth.
Mount Zion — the summit of Zaphon — 
is the city of the great King. ⓗ
CSB17.3:
God is known as a stronghold
in its citadels. ⓘ
CSB17.4:
Look! The kings assembled;
they advanced together. ⓙ
CSB17.5:
They looked and froze with fear;
they fled in terror. ⓚ
CSB17.6:
Trembling seized them there,
agony like that of a woman in labor, ⓛ
CSB17.7:
as you wrecked the ships of Tarshish
with the east wind. ⓜ
CSB17.8:
Just as we heard, so we have seen
in the city of the LORD of Armies,
in the city of our God;
God will establish it forever. ⓝ Selah
CSB17.9:
God, within your temple,
we contemplate your faithful love. ⓐ
CSB17.10:
Like your name, God, so your praise
reaches to the ends of the earth; ⓑ
your right hand is filled with justice. ⓒ
CSB17.11:
Mount Zion is glad.
Judah’s villages [1] rejoice
because of your judgments. ⓓ
CSB17.12:
Go around Zion, encircle it;
count its towers,
CSB17.13:
note its ramparts; tour its citadels
so that you can tell a future generation: ⓔ
CSB17.14:
“This God, our God forever and ever —
he will always lead us.” [2] ⓕ

AMP.1:
A Song; a Psalm of the sons of Korah. Great is the LORD, and greatly to be praised,
In the city of our God, His holy mountain.
AMP.2:
Fair and beautiful in elevation, the joy of all the earth,
Is Mount Zion [the City of David] in the far north,
The city of the great King.
AMP.3:
God, in her palaces,
Has made Himself known as a stronghold.
AMP.4:
For, lo, the kings assembled themselves,
They [came and] passed by together.
AMP.5:
They saw it, then they were amazed;
They were stricken with terror, they fled in alarm.
AMP.6:
Panic seized them there,
And pain, as that of a woman in childbirth.
AMP.7:
With the east wind
You shattered the ships of Tarshish.
AMP.8:
As we have heard, so have we seen
In the city of the LORD of hosts, in the city of our God:
God will establish her forever. Selah.
AMP.9:
We have thought of Your lovingkindness, O God,
In the midst of Your temple.
AMP.10:
As is Your name, O God,
So is Your praise to the ends of the earth;
Your right hand is full of righteousness (rightness, justice).
AMP.11:
Let Mount Zion be glad,
Let the daughters of Judah rejoice
Because of Your [righteous] judgments.
AMP.12:
Walk about Zion, go all around her;
Count her towers,
AMP.13:
Consider her ramparts,
Go through her palaces,
That you may tell the next generation [about her glory].
AMP.14:
For this is God,
Our God forever and ever;
He will be our guide even until death.

MSG.1:
A psalm of the sons of Korah
God majestic,
praise abounds in our God-city!
His sacred mountain,
MSG.2:
breathtaking in its heights—earth's joy.
Zion Mountain looms in the North,
city of the world-King.
MSG.4:
The kings got together,
they united and came.
MSG.5:
They took one look and shook their heads,
they scattered and ran away.
MSG.6:
They doubled up in pain
like a woman having a baby.
MSG.7:
You smashed the ships of Tarshish
with a storm out of the East.
MSG.8:
We heard about it, then we saw it
with our eyes—
In God's city of angel armies,
in the city our God
Set on firm foundations,
firm forever.
MSG.9:
We pondered your love-in-action, God,
waiting in your temple:
MSG.10:
Your name, God, evokes a train
of Hallelujahs wherever
It is spoken, near and far;
your arms are heaped with goodness-in-action.
MSG.11:
Be glad, Zion Mountain;
Dance, Judah's daughters!
He does what he said he'd do!
MSG.12:
Circle Zion, take her measure,
count her fortress peaks,
MSG.13:
Gaze long at her sloping bulwark,
climb her citadel heights—
Then you can tell the next generation
detail by detail the story of God,
MSG.14:
Our God forever,
who guides us till the end of time.

NIV.1:
Psalm 48
A song. A psalm of the Sons of Korah.
Great is the Lord, and most worthy of praise,
in the city of our God, his holy mountain.
NIV.2:
It is beautiful in its loftiness,
the joy of the whole earth.
Like the utmost heights of Zaphon is Mount Zion,
the city of the Great King.
NIV.3:
God is in her citadels;
he has shown himself to be her fortress.
NIV.4:
When the kings joined forces,
when they advanced together,
NIV.5:
they saw her and were astounded;
they fled in terror.
NIV.6:
Trembling seized them there,
pain like that of a woman in labor.
NIV.7:
You destroyed them like ships of Tarshish
shattered by an east wind.
NIV.8:
As we have heard,
so have we seen
in the city of the Lord Almighty,
in the city of our God:
God makes her secure forever. Selah
NIV.9:
Within your temple, O God,
we meditate on your unfailing love.
NIV.10:
Like your name, O God,
your praise reaches to the ends of the earth;
your right hand is filled with righteousness.
NIV.11:
Mount Zion rejoices,
the villages of Judah are glad
because of your judgments.
NIV.12:
Walk about Zion, go around her,
count her towers,
NIV.13:
consider well her ramparts,
view her citadels,
that you may tell of them to the next generation.
NIV.14:
For this God is our God for ever and ever;
he will be our guide even to the end.

NRSVCE.1:
Great is the LORD and greatly to be praised
in the city of our God.
His holy mountain,
NRSVCE.2:
beautiful in elevation,
is the joy of all the earth,
Mount Zion, in the far north,
the city of the great King.
NRSVCE.3:
Within its citadels God
has shown himself a sure defense.
NRSVCE.4:
Then the kings assembled,
they came on together.
NRSVCE.5:
As soon as they saw it, they were astounded;
they were in panic, they took to flight;
NRSVCE.6:
trembling took hold of them there,
pains as of a woman in labor,
NRSVCE.7:
as when an east wind shatters
the ships of Tarshish.
NRSVCE.8:
As we have heard, so have we seen
in the city of the LORD of hosts,
in the city of our God,
which God establishes forever. Selah
NRSVCE.9:
We ponder your steadfast love, O God,
in the midst of your temple.
NRSVCE.10:
Your name, O God, like your praise,
reaches to the ends of the earth.
Your right hand is filled with victory.
NRSVCE.11:
Let Mount Zion be glad,
let the towns of Judah rejoice
because of your judgments.
NRSVCE.12:
Walk about Zion, go all around it,
count its towers,
NRSVCE.13:
consider well its ramparts;
go through its citadels,
that you may tell the next generation
NRSVCE.14:
that this is God,
our God forever and ever.
He will be our guide forever.

NET.1: The LORD is great and certainly worthy of praise in the city of our God, his holy hill.
NET.2: It is lofty and pleasing to look at, a source of joy to the whole earth. Mount Zion resembles the peaks of Zaphon; it is the city of the great king.
NET.3: God is in its fortresses; he reveals himself as its defender.
NET.4: For look, the kings assemble; they advance together.
NET.5: As soon as they see, they are shocked; they are terrified, they quickly retreat.
NET.6: Look at them shake uncontrollably, like a woman writhing in childbirth.
NET.7: With an east wind you shatter the large ships.
NET.8: We heard about God’s mighty deeds, now we have seen them, in the city of the LORD, the invincible Warrior, in the city of our God. God makes it permanently secure. (Selah)
NET.9: We reflect on your loyal love, O God, within your temple.
NET.10: The praise you receive as far away as the ends of the earth is worthy of your reputation, O God. You execute justice!
NET.11: Mount Zion rejoices; the towns of Judah are happy, because of your acts of judgment.
NET.12: Walk around Zion! Encircle it! Count its towers!
NET.13: Consider its defenses! Walk through its fortresses, so you can tell the next generation about it!
NET.14: For God, our God, is our defender forever! He guides us!
